data.title: Federal Reserve plans to cut workforce by ~10% mainly through attrition - report
data.description:
The Federal Reserve is planning to reduce its headcount by about 10% in the next couple of years, mainly through attrition, Bloomberg News reported May 16, citing a memo by the Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

The central bank is set to open a voluntary deferred resignation program to employees fully eligible to retire as of Dec. 31, 2027. The program will be effective across the Fed system - including the Board of Governors in Washington and the 12 regional reserve banks, the report noted.

The report comes amid a potential wider scrutiny of costs at the central bank. The Trump administration has reportedly been pressuring federal agencies to reduce headcount and streamline operations.

The 2024 budget foresaw an increase in the number of employees across the Fed system to 24,553 from 23,950 in the central bank's 2023 annual report. However, Powell denies that the central bank is overstaffed, Bloomberg noted.

"I have directed the leadership of the Federal Reserve, here at the Board and across the System, to find incremental ways to consolidate functions where appropriate, modernize some business practices and ensure that we are right-sized and able to meet our statutory mission," Powell reportedly said in the memo.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

Market-tracking ETFs include: (SPY), (QQQ), (DIA), (VOO), (IVV), (DOG) (SQQQ), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), and (QLD).

data.title: Archer Aviation's 50% rally has Wall Street watching the skies
data.description:
A trifecta of strong Q1 results, improved liquidity, and strategic partnerships sent Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) surging 50% this week, marking the stock’s best performance since its public offering in September 2021.

“2025 is an inflection point for Archer,” the company’s CEO Adam Goldstein said during the Q1 earnings call, boasting that “Archer is pushing the boundaries of what’s possible and reshaping the future of aviation for years to come.”

Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) is among the top five companies developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for use as air taxis. The company is developing a piloted, four-passenger aircraft called Midnight that is designed to perform back-to-back flights with minimal charge time in between. Archer (NYSE:ACHR) aims to deploy Midnight in the UAE later this year with the support of the Abu Dhabi government, sovereign wealth funds, and regulatory partnership with the UAE General Civil Aviation Authority (“GCAA”). The company also plans a second launch with Ethiopian Airlines that will give Archer a foothold in East Africa’s rapidly growing aviation market.

Closer to home, Archer Aviation (ACHR) was selected as the Official Air Taxi Provider of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympic and Paralympic Games, exposing the company to more than 15 million visitors and global viewership in the billions.

With pillars intact for global expansion, the only thing missing for Archer (ACHR) is a profit. The company’s Q1 results again showed no revenue, with an adjusted loss of $93.4M. But fortunately for a development-stage company like Archer (ACHR), cash burn is a more critical metric, and on this front, Archer (ARCH) is outperforming.

The company ended the quarter with its highest-ever quarter end balance of $1.03B, excluding an additional $400M from Stellantis (STLA).

“The company posted an EBITDA loss of $109 million, but a >$1 billion cash balance mitigates future cash burn risks,” said Seeking Alpha analyst Dhierin Bechai, investing group leader for The Aerospace Forum, adding that the investment in staffing and materials “is a good sign that shows the company has confidence in its pathway to commercial service.”

The company is also monetizing its aircraft before certification with its Launch Edition program, deploying the Midnight aircraft in “early adopter markets” like the UAE and Ethiopia, potentially generating millions of dollars in revenue.

"Archer continues to deepen high-impact partnerships across commercial aviation (United Airlines), defense (Anduril), and advanced software (Palantir), which collectively support credibility, operational scale, and long-term monetization across both civil and military use," Needham's Chris Pierce said earlier this week. 

But despite its earnings potential, lucrative strategic alliances, and meteoric gains in the company’s share price, Archer Aviation (ACHR) is not without its detractors.

Tariff risks, certification hurdles, and a frothy valuation that seems to be driven more by investors’ insatiable appetite for risk rather than fundamentals are at the top of the list, with some saying Archer’s (ARCH) order book is the only attractive part of the business.

“Archer has so far managed to provide a steady stream of positive news in support of the stock, but it has probably reached a point where real progress on flight operations and FAA certification is needed,” Seeking Alpha analyst Richard Durant says, attributing the company’s share price to investors' hopes that it will be the first to commercialize its aircraft and receive FAA certification.

Some say Archer's laser focus on being the first to market has led the company to lift designs from competitor Wisk (BA), scale back its design burden, and accelerate its path through certification.

“These choices involve tradeoffs, and it is generally accepted that Archer’s aircraft will be inferior to Joby’s across a number of metrics,” Durant adds.

Whichever player reaches FAA certification first -- be it Archer (ACHR), Joby (JOBY), or Wisk Aero (BA) – the financial reward is substantial. According to market research, the advanced air mobility market is projected to reach $29.8B by 2032 from $9.2B in 2025, driven by increasing urban congestion, advancements in electric propulsion, and improved battery systems.

data.title: Virgin Galactic soars +80% after re-igniting bulls with its strategy update
data.description:
Virgin Galactic (NYSE:SPCE) rallied sharply on Friday, due to a combination of better-than-expected Q1 earnings results, a narrower loss from a year ago, and positive updates on its future spaceflight plans and pricing strategies.

The space tourism company confirmed that it is on track to begin payload flights with its new Delta Class SpaceShips in summer 2026, with private astronaut flights scheduled for fall 2026. Virgin Galactic (NYSE:SPCE) also disclosed plans to raise ticket prices above the previous $600,000 mark for the upcoming flights, which analysts see as signaling confidence in strong future demand and improved profitability.

Virgin Galactic (SPCE) is also about halfway through a feasibility study for a potential second spaceport in Italy. During the earnings conference call, CEO Michael Colglazier said SPCE is starting with a target to be able to fly 125 flights over the course of the year.

TD Cowen  kot a Buy rating on Virgin Galactic (SPCE) following the report. "We expect SPCE to operate a highly profitable commercial spaceflight business with full-scale service inclusive of 3-400 passenger flights each year per spaceport," updated analyst Oliver Chen. He noted that Virgin Galactic (SPCE) has a first-mover advantage in a high-barrier-to-entry market and appears well-positioned to capitalize on strong demand from high-net-worth consumers. "SPCE's efficient manufacturing model, which leverages external suppliers for major spacecraft and carrier aircraft sub-assemblies should enable the company to explore the commercial human spaceflight market outside the U.S. over the long term," he added.

Shares of Virgin Galactic (SPCE) were up 81.6% at 1:18 p.m. and traded as high as $6.63 earlier in the session to mark a 2025 high. Short interest on SPCE stands at 26.3% of the total float, which may be adding to the volatility.

data.title: SWK Holdings outlines $20M annual finance segment target while advancing share repurchase and special dividend strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SWK Holdings Corporation (SWKH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Jody Staggs, President and CEO, noted strong financial segment profitability and the successful monetization of the majority of the royalty portfolio. Staggs stated, “First quarter SWK highlights include, $8.6 million of Finance segment adjusted non-GAAP net income, bringing the trailing 12-month total to $26 million, a new $15 million financing to an innovative life science company and continued partnership advancement between our MOD3 Pharma division and its strategic partner.”
  • Staggs shared that non-GAAP tangible financing book value per share grew to $21.73, achieving a 10% year-over-year growth, with MOD3 adding $0.38 per share for a total tangible book value per share of $22.11. Pro forma for the $4 per share special dividend, total tangible book value per share was $18.11.
  • Staggs highlighted, “Year-to-date, we have repurchased $1.1 million of our shares and with the stock trading at a discount to book value and given our excess capital, I expect the Board will authorize a new share repurchase program in coming days.”
  • Staggs indicated the gross Finance Receivables portfolio consisted of approximately $220 million of performing first lien loans and $13 million of nonaccruals, with a $9 million CECL reserve, and gross cash as of the call totaling approximately $22 million, with the revolving credit facility undrawn.
  • Staggs emphasized, “At March 31st, 2025, the Finance Receivable portfolio had an effective or modeled yield of 14.5%. So if the portfolio repays as modeled, it should generate approximately $32 million of annual interest income.”
  • The MOD3 CDMO division reported segment revenue of $1 million and segment EBITDA loss of $0.5 million for the quarter, with a $1.8 million option fee received from the strategic partner.
  • Adam Rice, CFO, stated, “We reported GAAP pretax net income of $5.8 million or $0.48 per diluted share. Our reported first quarter 2025 net income is $4.5 million after income tax expense of $1.3 million.”

Outlook

  • Staggs detailed a framework for go-forward profitability: “The current portfolio should generate approximately $32 million of interest income, if it repays as modeled. On the expense side, we are targeting approximately $8 million of normalized annual OpEx. The bond interest expense totaled $3 million and our revolver carrying cost is approximately $0.5 million. So a reasonable target is approximately $20 million of Finance segment adjusted non-GAAP net income, based on the current portfolio size. To be clear, this is not guidance and does not consider impairments, early payoffs, warrant gains, abnormal OpEx, additional deployments, et cetera, and is really just intended to provide a framework for how to think about go-forward profitability.”
  • Management indicated pursuit of additional financings and noted a competitive market for high-quality borrowers, stressing portfolio quality and mid-teens return targets.
  • Staggs stated, "We believe the portfolio remains strong and the most recent credit score reached an all-time high."

Financial Results

  • Rice reported a $300,000 decrease in Finance Receivables segment revenue and a $700,000 increase in pharmaceutical development segment revenue for Q1 2025.
  • GAAP book value per share was $23.94, a 6.8% increase compared to $22.42 as of March 31, 2024. Non-GAAP tangible book value per share totaled $21.73, a 10.5% increase from $19.66 as of March 31, 2024.
  • Overall operating expenses were $3.7 million in Q1 2025, down from $10.3 million in Q1 2024, attributed mainly to a decrease in provision for credit losses, which saw $1 million of asset impairments in Q1 2025 versus $6 million in Q1 2024.
  • MOD3 operating expenses were $1.5 million compared to $1.7 million in Q1 2024, and Finance Receivables segment operating expenses were $2.2 million versus $8.6 million a year earlier.
  • The company repurchased approximately 52,000 shares at $900,000 during the quarter and an additional 11,000 shares for $200,000 since quarter close.

Q&A

  • Unidentified Analyst congratulated the team and inquired about the two loans with a credit score of two totaling $20 million, asking if they were loans or royalties. Staggs answered, “Yes, those are both loans. So we have three -- the three nonaccruals are post re-org royalties... The two loans are still first lien term loans that where we have the full lean covenants, et cetera.”
  • The analyst asked about competition in the private credit space and market dynamics. Staggs responded, “Our space is still very interesting. The kind of $10 million to $25 million space. But there's other folks in our space... We've got to really be good partners, be proactive in those situations.”
  • The analyst questioned the carrying value of certain warrants and contingent rights. Staggs confirmed, “We have 12 discrete instruments... that are either private warrants or we've got you mentioned those CVR. We've got a couple of re-org tail payments. Those are all carried at zero... cumulatively, I think they are worth more than zero.”
  • Stefano Latapy, Cannell Capital, asked about best use of capital and future loan opportunities. Staggs stated, “I think it's buying back stock is really interesting here... We did paid a special dividend... And the third, I think, is selective additional loans, really keeping kind of right in our sweet spot.” On the loan pipeline, Staggs said, “The pipeline is probably, I would say, roughly the same as it's been over the past year. It's not, I'll call it neutral. It's not extremely attractive like it might have been 75 days ago, 60 days ago. But there are still some opportunities...”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed positive sentiment, repeatedly congratulating management and acknowledging progress, particularly regarding capital return and loan performance.
  • Management’s tone was confident during both prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing portfolio strength and proactive capital management, while acknowledging competitive market pressures and the need for selectivity in new financings.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a confident stance, reinforced by tangible book value growth and capital return announcements, while analysts remained supportive and positive in tone.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company advanced from discussing an anticipated special dividend in Q4 2024 to having executed a $4 per share special dividend in Q1 2025.
  • Tangible book value per share continued to rise, supported by ongoing share repurchases and consistent growth in core financial metrics.
  • Portfolio credit quality and yield metrics remained robust, though modeled yield slightly decreased from 15.5% in Q4 2024 to 14.5% in Q1 2025.
  • Management’s focus shifted from executing the royalty portfolio sale and FX hedge in Q4 to emphasizing framework targets for annual profitability and capital deployment strategies in Q1.
  • Analyst questions remained focused on capital allocation, credit portfolio quality, and return of capital, showing ongoing interest in risk mitigation and shareholder returns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing monitoring of healthcare and general economic regulatory changes but stated, “At this time, we don't believe any of these changes pose outsized risk to our portfolio.”
  • Staggs discussed the presence of three nonaccruals and two loans with lower credit scores, emphasizing active dialogue with borrowers and conservative risk marking for private warrants and contingent rights.
  • The competitive landscape for high-quality borrowers was highlighted as requiring selectivity and creativity to maintain portfolio quality and targeted returns.

Final Takeaway

Management underscored a successful first quarter marked by robust profitability, a strengthened balance sheet, and the execution of a special dividend alongside share repurchases. The company remains focused on disciplined capital allocation, maintaining portfolio quality, and selectively deploying capital, while providing a transparent framework for future profitability and actively addressing both risks and opportunities within its core markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA Asks: Will Apple move iPhone production to U.S. rather than India?
data.description:
On Thursday, President Trump said he was disappointed with Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) decision to move iPhone manufacturing from China to India rather than the United States.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is in the process of migrating certain manufacturing operations to India in part to mitigate increased tariffs on Chinese-produced goods. But given Trump's reaction to the move, will Apple (APPL) decide to shift operations to the U.S. instead?

Seeking Alpha analysts Bill Maurer, Geneva Investor and Oliver Rodzianko offer their thoughts on the situation. 

Bill Maurer: I don't think Apple is going to change its plans just because of President Trump's comments. Not only would producing iPhones in the U.S. likely be extremely costly and thus impact consumers, but the current president may no longer run the country by the time Apple (AAPL) would be able to produce the smartphone domestically. Apple (AAPL) has spent years working to increase production in many places outside of China, including India, and I don't think it will abandon those plans based on a political environment that could easily change in a few years or even at the 2026 midterms.

Geneva Investor: I do not think Apple (AAPL) will radically change course on India manufacturing. Apple’s (AAPL) effort to produce iPhones in India dates to before the Trump administration. I see it as a general effort to diversify away from China, which was hit by tariffs even before the current U.S. administration and by other countries, too. With ~60% of Apple’s (AAPL) sales generated outside of the U.S., Apple (AAPL) has all the interest to diversify its supply chain.

Additionally, producing such sophisticated devices in America is not economically feasible for Apple (AAPL). Bank of America estimates that an American-produced iPhone could cost up to 90% more to make. China’s current tariffs, at 30%, are far below that amount. India’s 10% are very manageable. Unless Trump ends up de facto embargoing both China and India, Apple (AAPL) is better off not producing in America.

Not to mention that this administration has created a very uncertain future when it comes to the extent and duration of tariffs. Apple (AAPL) cannot embark on such a long-term CAPEX investment without knowing whether these tariffs will even remain in place. Even if tariffs were to be raised and maintained, there would be a significant risk of roll-back after 2026 midterm elections or 2028. I find it more likely that Apple (AAPL) will commit to some other form of investment in America to “appease” Trump, so that the administration can present it as a win.

Oliver Rodzianko: Apple (AAPL) will likely stick to its India manufacturing strategy, even though it has been pressured by President Trump to bring manufacturing back home. After all, Apple (AAPL) needs to avoid near-term margin compression. 

Foxconn's (OTCPK:FXCOF) expansion in India essentially confirms this: Apple (AAPL) will more than double iPhone manufacturing there, as Foxconn (OTCPK:FXCOF) aims for 25–30 million units in 2025. This permits resilience and cost efficiency, whereas a sharp U.S. turn would be costlier due to labor and property costs. Apple (AAPL) also has regulatory concerns with its high-margin Services segment, so it needs to do all it can to maintain its overall profitability profile right now. 

An American manufacturing boom is still a longer-term possibility, pending automation advances. Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) is constructing new American chip fabs, but Apple's (AAPL) re-shoring will wait for robotics to reach maturity. Tim Cook says iPhones won't be built in the U.S. until robotic arms have the ability to work "at scale and precision.” As automation via AI closes labor cost disparities in five to 15 years, Apple can ramp up U.S. operations, boosting growth and making our global supply chains that much more secure.

data.title: EOG Resources wins oil exploration concession for UAE shale block
data.description:
EOG Resources (NYSE:EOG) said Friday it was awarded a new onshore oil exploration concession for Unconventional Onshore Block 3 in the United Arab Emirates, where it expects to begin drilling in this year's H2.

EOG (NYSE:EOG) will hold a 100% interest in the block and will be the operator, but will coordinate with Abu Dhabi National Oil Company - ADNOC - to explore and appraise unconventional oil in the concession area, and following a three-year appraisal phase, EOG said it may enter into a production concession in which ADNOC has the option to participate.

The UCO3 concession area covers nearly 900K acres in "an over-pressured, oil prone basin" in the UAE's Al Dhafra region of Abu Dhabi.

ADNOC said the award is the first of its kind to a U.S. company and "underlines the attractiveness of Abu Dhabi’s energy sector and its position as a trusted investment destination."

The agreement was one of a number of business deals agreed between the U.S. and UAE during President Trump's visit this week.

data.title: Fifth Third Bancorp explores ways to expand crypto business - report
data.description:
Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ:FITB) is weighing a deeper push into crypto-related services, including stablecoin-based cross-border transfers and integration with trading and payment networks, according to a media report dated Friday.

The Cincinnati, Ohio-based lender has already established a niche client base that uses its banking infrastructure for functions like payroll and revenue collection, Ben Hoffman, the company's chief strategy officer, told Bloomberg in an interview.

Other areas of exploration include using stablecoins to facilitate international transactions to reduce costs and boost overall efficiency, he added, noting the company is also looking to link into payment networks that support consumers' crypto purchases and digital asset trading.

The move comes after five years of Fifth Third (NASDAQ:FITB) waiting for crypto regulation to become clearer, Hoffman said, Bloomberg reported. Now, with pro-crypto U.S. President Donald Trump implementing friendly crypto rules, 'it became clear that this was the right time to engage and fortunately we’ve had a team of folks actively studying and tinkering in this space,” he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Adobe rises as Jefferies sees estimates as 'conservative,' updates Creative Cloud
data.description:
• Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) shares rose more than 3% on Friday after investment firm Jefferies said it sees earnings estimates as “conservative.”  
  • "We are positive on ADBE's new price increase of $10 (up 11-17%) on Creative Cloud All Apps plans for both individuals and teams, effective on 6/17 or at renewal,” analyst Brent Thill wrote in a note to clients. “While already embedded in FY25 guide, these increase confidence in hitting rev guide 8.9% growth at mid-pt vs. 10.8% in FY24. These should complement Firefly plans ($10-200/[month]) and enhance ADBE's role in AI.”
  • Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) shares rose more than 3% on Friday after investment firm Jefferies said it sees earnings estimates as “conservative.”  
  • Thill has a Buy rating on Adobe and $590 price target on the stock. 
  • In addition, Adobe announced changes to its Creative Cloud subscription offering for teachers and students in the U.S.

data.title: FCC approves Verizon's purchase of Frontier Communications
data.description:
• The Federal Communications Commission approved Verizon's (NYSE:VZ) $20 billion purchase of Frontier Communication (NASDAQ:FYBR) after the telecom firm agreed to end its diversity, equity and inclusion policies. 
  • The FCC approved the transfer of licenses, according to a filing with the regulator. 
  • FCC Commissioner Brendan Carr had threatened in recent months to block deals from companies that promote DEI policies.

data.title: Apple Pay, Card, Cash all having issues
data.description:
• Apple Pay and Apple Card are both experiencing issues, as well as Apple Cash according to Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) status monitor page. 
  • All three services have yellow diamonds next to them on the page, signaling an issue.
